summary: Excellent matrix recording captures a stellar 1991 show highlighted by an epic Dark Star and a powerful second set.
review: The matrix blend of board and audience sources is praised as “killer” and “masterful,” delivering crystal‑clear sound that lets the band’s improvisations shine. Fans rave about the 38‑minute Dark Star, the fiery Help > Slipknot! > Franklin's sequence, and a second set that feels “real treat.” While one reviewer lambasted the keyboards, the overall consensus is that this performance ranks among the best of the ’91 run.

ai_rating.rationale: Average user rating is ~4.1 stars; reviewers overwhelmingly praise the musical performance and matrix recording quality, with only isolated criticism of keyboards.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Highly regarded matrix mix that blends a clean board feed with audience ambience; praised as “killer” and “masterful,” no major drop‑outs or muddiness reported.

standout_songs: Help on the Way > Slipknot! > Franklin's Tower
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set features rare tunes, but the second set is where the show shines—strong close, intense jams, and a memorable Dark Star that carries the energy.

band_member_comments.Keys: Several reviewers criticize Hornsby’s out‑of‑tune playing and Vince’s sax/keyboard contributions, calling the keys a “spotlight of lameness” in parts of Dark Star.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drums are described as sublime, especially when paired with the front three and Branford.
